This is not a "Claude Code is bad, everyone should switch" article. I still use Claude Code at work, and if cost were less of a fa
articles.content: Introduction I've been seeing more developers say that Codex has become easier to use, more cost-effective, or simply a better fit for some workflows than it used to be. This is not a "Claude Code is bad, everyone should switch" article. I still use Claude Code at work, and if cost were less of a factor in my personal setup, I would probably be using both more actively. If you're already comfortable with Claude Code and you're wondering whether Codex is worth trying too, this guide covers the th
articles.author: K9i - Kota Hayashi

articles.title: The 7 Places Agency Client Onboarding Breaks (And How to Fix Them)
articles.description: 1. The access collection black hole You need Figma access, Google Analytics, WordPress admin, GitHub, and the client's Slack. You ask. They forward a password email from two years ago. You ask again. Their developer says they'll get back to you. Three days pass. The fix: Send a single, complete acce
articles.content: 1. The access collection black hole You need Figma access, Google Analytics, WordPress admin, GitHub, and the client's Slack. You ask. They forward a password email from two years ago. You ask again. Their developer says they'll get back to you. Three days pass. The fix: Send a single, complete access list on Day 1 — not "we'll need some access" but the exact list, with specifics for each tool, and a deadline. Include what happens if the deadline is missed. Framing it as "we start discovery with
articles.author: Lisa Sakura

articles.content: Way back in the DS days, Nintendo decided to stop selling to Amazon. During a recent lecture at NYU , former Nintendo of America President Reggie Fils-Aimé said it was because Amazon was seeking preferential treatment that would have hurt its relationship with other retailers, and potentially broken the law . The two sides have since made amends , and you can buy a Switch 2 through Amazon. But for a long time, Nintendo consoles had been largely unavailable on the site. In the 2000s, Amazo

articles.title: Research roundup: 6 cool science stories we almost missed
articles.description: Crushing soda cans for science, why dolphins swim so fast, how urine helps mushrooms communicate, and more
articles.content: It’s a regrettable reality that there is never enough time to cover all the interesting scientific stories we come across. So every month, we highlight a handful of the best stories that nearly slipped through the cracks. April’s list includes tracking Roman ship repairs, the discovery that mushrooms can detect human urine, crushing soda cans for science, and the physics of why dolphins can swim so fast. Physics of why dolphins swim so fast Dolphins are very good swimmers but the exact mechanism
articles.author: Jennifer Ouellette

articles.title: Infrasound waves stop kitchen fires, but can they replace sprinklers?
articles.description: Acoustic fire suppression goes commercial.
articles.content: In a makeshift demonstration kitchen in Concord, California, cooking oil splatters in and around a frying pan, which catches fire on an unattended gas stove. Within moments, a smoke detector wails. But in this demonstration, something less common happens: An AI-driven sensor activates and wall emitters blast infrasound waves toward the source of the fire in an attempt to put it out. The science of acoustic fire suppression, which has long been known and documented in scientific literature and th
articles.author: Cyrus Farivar
